That's that glistening surface you see on the lung.
The only time we ever remove that is in an operation called a decortication, where people have what you call a trapped lung, often from infection that you call empyema.
And then we have to scrape that stuff off the lung to get that lung to open back up.
But in that case, it helps their lung because we get the lung to expand where it's been trapped.
